behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is primarily classification/handoff logic (git-change parser -> category aggregator), which can fail across module boundaries even when unit tests pass. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement to validate real subprocess contract/exit semantics; a deterministic fixture of rename entries can reproduce the failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text only requires category correctness, not explicit verification of real git response contract or external round-trip side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Run the parser+aggregation pipeline end-to-end in-process with mixed change fixtures containing rename records and assert renamed count increments while added/deleted counts do not include those renamed paths.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: crash risk often appears when diff parsing and report generation interact on binary markers. REAL-INFRA: behavior does not explicitly require validating real subprocess error/happy-path contract details; representative binary-diff text fixtures can expose parser crash/count bugs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text asks for robustness/counting, not explicit external contract checks (status/body/exit-code/error-shape) or explicit round-trip side-effect verification.
behaviors.test_description: Execute parsing + reporting modules together on fixture diffs that include binary file entries (e.g., binary patch markers) and assert process completes and binary file counts are present.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/SYSTEM TRIAD: this behavior targets a real external error path from git in an unborn-HEAD repo; correctness depends on handling actual subprocess exit/error semantics and still producing valid stdout report. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing git can hide real no-commit failure modes. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo with no commits, run the CLI as a subprocess, assert zero exit status (or documented non-failing behavior), and verify stdout contains an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE + REAL-INFRA: this is an externally visible correctness claim against real repository history; validating only mocked git responses risks false confidence about real CLI/git interaction. SYSTEM TRIAD: explicit happy-path external contract verification (CLI output reflects real repo history) requires running against a real repo and reading stdout.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real temp repository with known commit/change history (add/delete/rename/modify), run the CLI subprocess, parse stdout report, and assert counts exactly match independently computed ground truth from that repo history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: staged vs unstaged semantics depend on real git index/working-tree behavior, which mocks can misrepresent. MOCK-HIDE: fake repositories often miss edge cases in index state transitions. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, create equivalent content changes once as staged and once as unstaged, run the CLI subprocess for each scenario, and assert normalized stdout reports are identical.
